title: astragalus pictus
intro: Astragalus pictus, also known as the painted milk vetch or painted milkvetch, is a perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of the western United States and Canada. It is a type of legume, belonging to the pea family (Fabaceae), and is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its adaptogenic and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Astragalus pictus include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce stress and fatigue, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory conditions such as bronchitis and asthma. Astragalus pictus can be prepared as an herbal supplement, including capsules, teas, and tinctures, as well as being used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions.
